title: Local AI Is Free—But There’s a Catch (Hybrid Setup Fix)
summary: Keith AI discusses the apparent cost savings of local AI while acknowledging hidden limitations and challenges. The video proposes a hybrid setup as a solution to address the practical constraints of running AI models locally.
writeup: A short walkthrough of a personal hybrid LLM routing setup that uses a local model as the default to avoid API costs, then falls back to cloud providers for speed-sensitive or tool-heavy work. The author routes Hermes and Open Claw through a local model by default, with OpenAI and Open Router as explicit fallbacks governed by task-type instructions. The core insight is that routing logic, not a single model choice, is the practical lever for balancing cost and latency.

key_points: Running a local model as the default eliminates API costs for lightweight tasks like research, summaries, and writing, while cloud fallback handles the cases where waiting is impractical.
The setup uses Hermes and Open Claw on a local model as the primary, with OpenAI and Open Router configured as fallback providers, so the switch is automatic rather than manual.

Routing instructions specify that coding tasks and workflows with many tool calls go to the cloud, while low-overhead tasks stay local, making the division based on task structure rather than a blanket preference.
